title: Aureline managed API / contract mismatch state record
description: Boundary schema for the contract_mismatch_state_record frozen in docs/runtime/managed_contract_mismatch_contract.md. The record exists so the desktop UI, CLI surfaces, diagnostics/doctor probes, service-health aggregator, support-bundle exporter, and release/compat tooling can agree on one typed description of managed API contract skew, provider schema drift, stale contract caches, browser/companion/runtime mismatches, and mirror-delayed metadata. The record is intentionally explicit about expected versus observed contract versions, the declared supported range, the local-safe fallback posture, and whether write paths are narrowed or blocked. Raw URLs, raw hostnames, raw tenant identifiers, raw access tokens, and raw payload bytes MUST NOT cross this boundary; the record carries opaque ids and typed vocabulary only. The eventual runtime contract-mismatch publisher crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new mismatch-domain class, mismatch-state class, fallback posture, read/write posture, suggested-action class, or service-contract-state projection is additive-minor and MUST bump contract_mismatch_state_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a governance decision row.

contract_mismatch_state_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this contract-mismatch state export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator. Always contract_mismatch_state_record for payloads conforming to this schema.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests, diagnostics, support bundles, and admin exports.

stable_ref.description: Stable lowercase reference token. It may use '.', ':', '_', or '-' separators.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short reviewer-facing sentence safe for UI tables, logs, and exports. Raw secret material and raw payload bytes MUST NOT appear.

boundary_family.description: Re-exported boundary-family vocabulary aligned with schemas/compat/mixed_version_envelope.schema.json. Included so contract-mismatch states can join to compat rows and skew windows when available.

compat_row_ref.description: Optional reference to a compat_row id from artifacts/compat/qualification_matrix_seed.yaml when the mismatch is rooted in a governed boundary row.

skew_register_ref.description: Optional reference to a skew_register id from artifacts/compat/version_skew_register.yaml when the mismatch is rooted in a governed boundary row.

skew_window_ref.description: Optional reference to a skew_window id from artifacts/compat/skew_windows.yaml when the mismatch is rooted in a governed boundary row.

supported_range_row_ref.description: Reference to a supported-range row id from artifacts/runtime/supported_range_rows.yaml. Used so supported ranges remain exportable and mechanically auditable.

mismatch_domain_class.description: High-level mismatch-domain taxonomy that drives user cues and repair routing.

mismatch_state_class.description: Closed mismatch-state vocabulary. supported_range_within_window captures current-plus-previous-minor / additive window cases where the contract is supported but not an exact match; surfaces MUST avoid implying full parity when this value applies.

supported_range_source_class.description: Where the supported-range declaration came from. cached_last_known_good and mirrored_registry are admissible offline; unresolved_unknown is explicit and non-conforming as a silent default.

fallback_posture_class.description: Closed fallback-posture vocabulary. degraded_* values require explicit disclosure of what remains safe locally; blocked_* values require an export/help action.

io_posture_class.description: Read/write posture class used for action-level denial or downgrade surfaces. normal means the mismatch does not narrow the lane; narrowed_* means the lane continues with restrictions; blocked means the lane is refused.

service_contract_state.description: Projection onto the shared service-contract-state vocabulary (see docs/adr/0013-docs-help-service-health-truth.md).

suggested_action_class.description: Closed suggested-action vocabulary. Surfaces may render a subset, but the record MUST name at least one action whenever mismatch_state_class is not supported_range_exact_match or no_mismatch.

window_class.description: Window class declared by the supported-range row (for example current_plus_previous_minor_or_lts, declared_adjacent_window, same_schema_epoch_additive_only).

min_supported_version.description: Minimum supported contract version when representable as a scalar. Null is admissible when the row declares a window class that is not reducible to scalar min/max without a companion release index.

max_supported_version.description: Maximum supported contract version when representable as a scalar. Null is admissible when the row declares a window class that is not reducible to scalar min/max without a companion release index.

range_label.description: Short human-readable label suitable for UI chips and exports (for example 'current + previous minor', 'same schema epoch (additive only)').

as_of.description: When the supported range snapshot was captured. Null is admissible only for embedded_default.

supported_range_snapshot.description: Snapshot of the supported-range row as applied to this mismatch evaluation. Captured so exports remain self-contained even when the row changes later.

compat_row_ref.description: Optional compat_row ref when the mismatch is tied to a governed compat boundary. Null is admissible for purely local cache drift or mirror-only metadata where no compat row exists yet.

skew_register_ref.description: Optional skew-register ref when the mismatch is tied to a governed compat boundary.

skew_window_ref.description: Optional skew-window ref when the mismatch is tied to a governed compat boundary.

expected_contract_version.description: Contract version the consumer expected to speak. Must be stable and displayable; raw build metadata belongs in build identity records, not here.

observed_contract_version.description: Contract version observed from the provider. Null is admissible only when the provider could not be contacted and only cached range evidence is available.

service_contract_state_projection.description: Projection onto the shared service-contract-state vocabulary used by service-health. contract_mismatch is used when the provider is reachable but outside the supported window; local_only is used when the provider is unreachable but the local-safe baseline applies.

suggested_actions.description: Ordered list of suggested repair, export, or continue-locally actions. Surfaces may render a subset but MUST NOT invent actions outside this vocabulary.

notes.description: Optional additional reviewer note. Never the sole carrier of supported-range or fallback semantics.

contract_mismatch_state_record.description: Contract mismatch state record: explicit, exportable truth about contract skew and the resulting local-safe fallback posture.
